steps.title: Identify the angle relationship at the intersection
steps.content: When line $k$ intersects line $n$, four angles are formed at the intersection point. The angle marked $x°$ (positioned upper-left of the intersection) and the angle marked \$145°$ (positioned lower-right) are vertical angles — the pair of opposite angles created when two lines intersect.

steps.title: Apply the vertical angles theorem
steps.content:
Vertical angles are always congruent. Since $x°$ and \$145°$ are vertical angles at the intersection of lines $k$ and $n$, they must be equal:

$$x = 145$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
